Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "Okonjo" or "Mehta" or "TN visa" or "H-1B" or "CMO") to find correspondence about the three relocations. Key discoveries: (a) Patricia Langford's April 8 employer letter for Dr. Kovacs -- contains a March 31 employment end date that is INCORRECT (actual departure is April 14); (b) Fatimah's April 9 reply to Patricia requesting a corrected letter aligning the end date with the April 14 departure -- this correction was NEVER received, leaving a critical documentation gap; (c) Lorraine Pham's urgent April 24 email to Fatimah stating she received NO communication from MoveOps about Nadia Okonjo's USCIS address change, despite being told MoveOps would coordinate -- the 10-day AR-11 filing window is closing or already passed; (d) Carmen Reyes's April 25 email reporting that the Pinnacle temporary housing option for Dr. Mehta is gone, The Langham Residences has a 24-hour hold expiring Saturday April 26 at 2:00 PM CT, and The Elysian remains available with no time pressure; (e) Sam Delgado's emails establishing Nadia Okonjo's H-1B status and attorney Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com); (f) Dr. Mehta's executive white-glove move details including chain-of-custody equipment and temporary housing for pregnant partner.
- Search Airtable relocations using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Fatimah Al-Rashidi") or airtable_list_records to find relocation records. Two records exist: recKovacsNashvilleToronto (Nashville to Toronto, In Progress) and recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin (SF to Austin, In Progress). NOTE: Dr. Mehta has NO Airtable relocation record -- the agent must discover this gap and create one via airtable_create_record. Read the Special Requirements fields to understand: (a) Kovacs -- customs broker BorderEase engaged, $850 surcharge pending client approval, employer letter needed by April 7; (b) Okonjo -- H-1B holder, critical constraint: "Do NOT schedule truck until written confirmation from attorney that USCIS filing is handled", visa expires June 15 2026, attorney is Lorraine Pham at Pham & Associates.
- Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Kovacs" or "Mehta" or "Okonjo" or "Fatimah") to find internal discussions. Key discoveries: (a) Catalina's April 9 message in #operations claiming she contacted immigration attorney Lorraine Pham on April 6 and that Lorraine confirmed awareness of the move -- this claim is CONTRADICTED by Lorraine's April 24 email (discovered via email search) saying she was never contacted by MoveOps; (b) Fatimah's consolidated status update on Dr. Mehta (temporary housing awaiting selection, chain-of-custody template blocker, Swift Relocations for household move); (c) Chloe's coordinator assignment messages; (d) Marcus's QuickBooks estimate breakdown (EST-2026-0422, $12,170 total MoveOps executive relocation package including $1,200 chain-of-custody surcharge, $800 specialty crating surcharge, $3,500 household move via Swift, and other line items); and (e) vendor quote status.
- Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (assignee: "moveops_fatimah_alrashidi" or query: "Kovacs" or "Okonjo" or "Mehta") to find tickets. Only Dr. Mehta has Linear tickets: #343 (Temporary Housing), #375 (Equipment/Chain-of-Custody), and #396 (Household Move), all assigned to Fatimah. NOTE: No Linear tickets exist for Dr. Kovacs or Nadia Okonjo -- the agent must discover this gap.
- Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices to find financial records. Discover INV-2026-0401 (Canopy Q2 wave, $9,210 with $2,650 in surcharges pending client approval: $850 customs broker for Kovacs, $1,200 cold-chain for Webb, $600 temp storage for Okonkwo). Also discover EST-2026-0422 (Dr. Mehta executive relocation estimate, $12,170 pending Canopy approval) -- the agent must explore its line items to find the $1,200 chain-of-custody surcharge and $800 specialty crating surcharge (2 x $400 for Eizo RadiForce monitors), which are DISTINCT from the $1,200 cold-chain surcharge for Webb on INV-2026-0401.
- Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina Dubois") to find catalina.dubois@moveops.com for the Nadia visa coordination email.
- Look up the #operations Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to find channel_id C006 for posting the summary.
- Update the existing Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01") for Kovacs (recKovacsNashvilleToronto) and Nadia (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin) with current status notes, outstanding items, and documentation gaps. Create a NEW Airtable record for Dr. Mehta using airtable_create_record since no record exists.
- Add comments to the three Dr. Mehta Linear tickets using linear_create_comment: linear_issue_bd446671ceed (Temporary Housing -- Pinnacle unit lost, Langham has 24-hour hold expiring April 26 2 PM CT, need selection ASAP), linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a (Equipment/Chain-of-Custody -- chain-of-custody template blocker, $800 specialty crating quote needed for calibrated monitors), and linear_issue_4902f5c68acb (Household Move -- Swift quote pending, delivery address blocked by housing selection). No Linear tickets exist for Kovacs or Nadia so no comments can be added for them.
- Post a summary message to #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006" or "#operations", payload: consolidated status summary of all three relocations) covering current status, outstanding items, and documentation gaps. CRITICAL: must flag the employer letter date discrepancy (March 31 employment end date vs April 14 departure) and that the corrected version requested April 9 was never received -- NOT mark it as complete. Also cover pending surcharges ($850 Kovacs customs, $1,200 Mehta chain-of-custody, $800 Mehta crating), vendor quote status, and the Nadia USCIS emergency so Chloe Vance can understand where each move stands.
- Send an email to Catalina Dubois using send_email (sender: "fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com", recipients: ["catalina.dubois@moveops.com"]) with an update on Nadia Okonjo's H-1B/USCIS coordination status. Must include: discovery of attorney Lorraine Pham (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com), the critical discrepancy between Catalina's April 9 Slack message claiming she contacted Lorraine on April 6 and Lorraine's April 24 email stating she received no communication from MoveOps, USCIS 10-day reporting window urgency, and asking Catalina to clarify whether she actually reached Lorraine.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "Okonjo" or "Mehta" or "TN visa" or "H-1B" or "CMO") to find correspondence about the three relocations. NOTE: Dr. Mehta has NO Airtable relocation record -- the agent must discover this gap and create one via airtable_create_record. Read the Special Requirements fields to understand: (a) Kovacs -- customs broker BorderEase engaged, $850 surcharge pending client approval, employer letter needed by April 7; (b) Okonjo -- H-1B holder, critical constraint: "Do NOT schedule truck until written confirmation from attorney that USCIS filing is handled", visa expires June 15 2026, attorney is Lorraine Pham at Pham & Associates.
- Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Kovacs" or "Mehta" or "Okonjo" or "Fatimah") to find internal discussions. Key discoveries: (a) Catalina's April 9 message in #operations claiming she contacted immigration attorney Lorraine Pham on April 6 and that Lorraine confirmed awareness of the move -- this claim is CONTRADICTED by Lorraine's April 24 email (discovered via email search) saying she was never contacted by MoveOps; (b) Fatimah's consolidated status update on Dr. Mehta (temporary housing awaiting selection, chain-of-custody template blocker, Swift Relocations for household move); (c) Chloe's coordinator assignment messages; (d) Marcus's QuickBooks estimate breakdown (EST-2026-0422, $12,170 total MoveOps executive relocation package including $1,200 chain-of-custody surcharge, $800 specialty crating surcharge, $3,500 household move via Swift, and other line items); and (e) vendor quote status.

NOTE: No Linear tickets exist for Dr. Kovacs or Nadia Okonjo -- the agent must discover this gap.
- Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices to find financial records. Discover INV-2026-0401 (Canopy Q2 wave, $9,210 with $2,650 in surcharges pending client approval: $850 customs broker for Kovacs, $1,200 cold-chain for Webb, $600 temp storage for Okonkwo). Also discover EST-2026-0422 (Dr. Mehta executive relocation estimate, $12,170 pending Canopy approval) -- the agent must explore its line items to find the $1,200 chain-of-custody surcharge and $800 specialty crating surcharge (2 x $400 for Eizo RadiForce monitors), which are DISTINCT from the $1,200 cold-chain surcharge for Webb on INV-2026-0401.
- Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina Dubois") to find catalina.dubois@moveops.com for the Nadia visa coordination email.
- Look up the #operations Slack channel using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to find channel_id C006 for posting the summary.
- Update the existing Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01") for Kovacs (recKovacsNashvilleToronto) and Nadia (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin) with current status notes, outstanding items, and documentation gaps. Create a NEW Airtable record for Dr. Mehta using airtable_create_record since no record exists.
- Add comments to the three Dr. Mehta Linear tickets using linear_create_comment: linear_issue_bd446671ceed (Temporary Housing -- Pinnacle unit lost, Langham has 24-hour hold expiring April 26 2 PM CT, need selection ASAP), linear_issue_8fe3ff7f9f9a (Equipment/Chain-of-Custody -- chain-of-custody template blocker, $800 specialty crating quote needed for calibrated monitors), and linear_issue_4902f5c68acb (Household Move -- Swift quote pending, delivery address blocked by housing selection). No Linear tickets exist for Kovacs or Nadia so no comments can be added for them.
- Post a summary message to #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006" or "#operations", payload: consolidated status summary of all three relocations) covering current status, outstanding items, and documentation gaps. CRITICAL: must flag the employer letter date discrepancy (March 31 employment end date vs April 14 departure) and that the corrected version requested April 9 was never received -- NOT mark it as complete. # Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., `airtable_search_records` or `airtable_list_records`) to retrieve relocation records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times (for Kovacs, Okonjo, and Mehta) to retrieve relocation records from the Relocations table in appMoveOpsOps001.
✓Run #2The agent called `airtable_search_records` multiple times (for Kovacs, Okonjo, and Mehta) and also `airtable_list_bases` and `airtable_list_tables` to retrieve Airtable records.
✓Run #3The agent used airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, and airtable_search_records (for Kovacs, Okonjo, and Mehta) to retrieve relocation records from Airtable.
✓Run #4The agent used multiple Airtable tools including `airtable_list_bases`, `airtable_list_tables`, `airtable_search_records` (with multiple queries for 'Kovacs', 'Okonjo', 'Mehta' on both 'Employee Name' and 'Name' fields), and `airtable_list_records` to retrieve all relocation records.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times (for Kovacs, Okonjo, Mehta by Name field, and for Chicago destination), and also called airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables to enumerate the database structure.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times (for Kovacs, Okonjo, Mehta, Anand, Dr. Anand Mehta) and also used airtable_list_tables, airtable_list_bases, and airtable_describe_table to explore the base structure. Airtable tools were clearly used.
2 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., `linear_list_issues` or `linear_get_issue`) to retrieve tickets for the relocations.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_issues with numerous queries: 'Kovacs', 'Okonjo', 'Mehta', 'customs border', 'visa immigration address', 'relocation', 'Nashville Toronto customs', 'PivotPoint', 'Nadia', and more. It successfully retrieved the Mehta tickets.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times (queries: Kovacs, Okonjo, Mehta, Nadia, Nashville Toronto, PivotPoint, H-1B USCIS, relocation, Toronto, Renata, customs, visa address) and also called linear_create_comment three times. Linear tools were clearly used.
3 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., `quickbooks_search_invoices`) to look up invoice or estimate data.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called `quickbooks_search_invoices` with criteria `{"DocNumber": "INV-2026-0401"}` to look up the Canopy invoice data.

23 The email to Catalina or the final response identifies the discrepancy between Catalina's April 9 Slack claim (that she contacted attorney Lorraine Pham on April 6 and received confirmation) and Lorraine Pham's April 24 email (stating she received no communication from MoveOps).
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent's email to Catalina says Lorraine 'has received NO contact from MoveOps since your initial heads-up,' implying Catalina's April 6 contact was acknowledged — when in fact Lorraine's email states she received NO communication from MoveOps at all. Neither the email nor the final response flags the contradiction between Catalina's Slack claim (that Lorraine 'confirmed she was aware') and Lorraine's flat denial of any MoveOps contact.
✗Run #2Neither the email to Catalina nor the final response explicitly flags the contradiction between Catalina's April 9 Slack claim (that she contacted Lorraine on April 6 and received confirmation) and Lorraine Pham's April 24 statement of zero communication from MoveOps. The agent frames the failure as Fatimah not following up with the destination address, accepting Catalina's account as accurate without questioning whether the initial contact actually occurred.
✓Run #3The email to Catalina explicitly identifies the contradiction: 'you noted that you'd already contacted Lorraine Pham and that she was expecting us' (Catalina's claim) versus 'Lorraine Pham sent me an urgent email stating: She has received NO communication from MoveOps' (Lorraine's April 24 email). The discrepancy is directly surfaced.
✗Run #4Neither the email to Catalina nor the final response identifies the contradiction between Catalina's April 9 Slack claim (that Lorraine confirmed awareness) and Lorraine's April 24 email (that she received no contact from MoveOps). The email to Catalina says Lorraine 'has received NO communication from MoveOps since your initial heads-up on April 6,' implying Lorraine did receive the initial heads-up — it doesn't flag that Catalina previously claimed Lorraine confirmed awareness, or that Lorraine denies receiving any contact at all.
✗Run #5The Slack message from Catalina (found in the trajectory) explicitly states she 'reached out to Lorraine Pham on April 6' and 'she confirmed she was aware.' Lorraine's April 24 email says zero contact from MoveOps. The agent's email to Catalina frames it as Catalina merely notifying Lorraine that Fatimah would follow up — not as Catalina having directly contacted Lorraine. The fundamental contradiction (Catalina claims she made contact; Lorraine says no one from MoveOps ever contacted her) is never explicitly surfaced in either the email to Catalina or the final response.
✗Run #6The email to Catalina says she 'confirmed on Slack (April 6) that she notified Lorraine the handoff was happening' and frames the failure as the ball being dropped on Fatimah's side. It does NOT identify the key contradiction: Catalina's Slack message also claimed Lorraine 'confirmed she was aware', which directly contradicts Lorraine's April 24 email stating she received zero communication from MoveOps. The discrepancy between Catalina's claim of confirmation and Lorraine's denial is never flagged in either the email or the final response.
